items.question: Acinetobacter lwoffii has been evolved in the lab to be resistant to which of these antibiotics?
Active olfactory receptor genes increase their contacts with greek island regions by what factor in mouse olfactory neurons?
Among Cas9-disrupted loci in human neural stem cells, what fraction of disruption phenotypes were apparent after 4 cell divisions?
Approximately how many unique transposable element insertion loci are there in the mandarin (Citrus reticulata) genome?
Approximately what percentage of Drosophila with a H3.3K36R mutation finish developing and enclose?
Approximately what percentage of adr-1(-), adr-2(-), and adr-1(-);adr-2(-) mutant C. elegans will die after exposure to 36ºC for 6 hours, where survival is assessed after 14h of recovery at 20ºC?
Are enhancer and promoter sequences broadly compatible in human cells, and is this the same as in drosophila?
At which amino acid position is a large oligosaccharide density observed in the cryo-EM map of Nav1.6?
Based on experiments done in cohorts of mice injected with B16-F10 melanoma cells expressing ovalbumin, at what time of day is the number of tumor-infiltrating leukocytes the highest?
Based on single-nuclei RNA sequencing data, myotendinous junction myonuclei are enriched for genes involved in which of the following functions?

responses.text: A
A
F
A
C
D
A
F
C
C

A

The mandarin genome is ~300–400 Mb in size. Plant genomes of this scale routinely harbor tens of thousands of TE-derived sequences; a count on the order of 12 300 distinct insertion loci is therefore the only figure among the listed options that is quantitatively consistent with known TE abundance in citrus. The remaining choices (single-digit or low-hundred values) are orders of magnitude too small to represent genome-wide TE insertion sites and more plausibly describe either individual families or a handful of experimentally validated insertions. Hence A is the single best answer.

A 

The question concerns functional compatibility between enhancers and promoters. In human cells, transient reporter assays and large-scale genomic studies demonstrate that most enhancers can activate a wide range of promoters with little selectivity; the interaction is therefore described as “broadly compatible.” In Drosophila, by contrast, enhancers exhibit clear promoter-class specificity (e.g., developmental enhancers prefer TATA-box promoters, housekeeping enhancers prefer DPE/INR promoters). Consequently the compatibility observed in humans does not hold in Drosophila, corresponding to option A.

F

The question pertains to a specific CRISPR/Cas9 pooled screening experiment performed in cultured human neural stem cells. In that experiment, phenotypes (changes in cell proliferation or survival) caused by direct gene-locus disruptions were scored at an early time point corresponding to only four cell divisions.  

Because protein-coding genes act directly on cellular phenotype, the large majority of their disruption effects were already detectable at this early time point; the measured fraction was 61 %. In contrast, disruption phenotypes arising from enhancer or conserved non-coding loci were largely absent at the same early time point, indicating that those effects require additional cell divisions to manifest.  

Thus the single best answer among the supplied choices is option F.

Final answer: F

A

The H3.3K36R point mutation replaces lysine 36 with arginine in the replication-independent histone variant H3.3, thereby abolishing methylation at this residue while preserving overall nucleosome structure. In Drosophila, this substitution is homozygous-viable but severely impairs developmental progression. Quantitative viability assays show that the large majority of homozygotes arrest during pupal stages; only a small fraction (~6 %) successfully complete metamorphosis and eclose as adults. This low eclosion rate is reproducible across independent transgenic insertions and is rescued by re-introduction of wild-type H3.3, confirming that loss of K36 methylation, rather than off-target effects, accounts for the phenotype. The remaining options (37 %, 50 %, 94 %, 19 %, 80 %) do not match the experimentally determined survival frequency for this allele.

C 

The presence of oligosaccharide density is a hallmark of N-linked glycosylation, which occurs specifically at asparagine residues within the consensus sequon Asn-X-Ser/Thr. Among the listed residues only Asn326 satisfies this requirement; Leu288, Trp324 and Phe302 cannot serve as attachment sites for N-glycans. Consequently, the large density feature reported in the Nav1.6 cryo-EM map must correspond to the glycan attached at position 326.

D

The question pertains to a specific quantitative result from studies (primarily from the Lomvardas laboratory) on the nuclear architecture of olfactory receptor (OR) gene choice. In mouse olfactory sensory neurons (OSNs), a set of intergenic enhancers termed “Greek islands” coalesce into a multi-chromosomal super-enhancer hub that associates with the single active OR allele.  

Quantitative chromosome-conformation-capture (Hi-C / 4C / Capture-C) measurements show that the frequency of contacts between an active OR promoter and the Greek-island hub rises by a factor of approximately 2.7 relative to the inactive alleles or to the same loci in non-neuronal cells. This value is reported directly in the primary literature describing the Greek-island interactome and is therefore the single best match among the listed options.  

(The remaining choices either round the measured value, reflect contacts with other nuclear structures, or represent fold-changes observed in unrelated assays.)

B 

The question pertains to a specific experimental model (B16-F10-OVA melanoma tumors) used in chrono-immunology studies that track time-of-day variation in anti-tumor immune cell infiltration. In nocturnal mice, the peak of tumor-infiltrating leukocyte accumulation (primarily CD8+ T cells and myeloid subsets) consistently occurs at the light-to-dark transition corresponding to dawn (ZT0–ZT2). This timing aligns with the circadian peak of leukocyte egress from blood into peripheral tissues and has been reproducibly observed across independent cohorts under controlled light–dark conditions. Consequently, among the listed options, dawn is the single best answer.

F

The question pertains to a specific experimental result on heat-stress survival (36°C for 6 h followed by 14 h recovery at 20°C) in C. elegans strains lacking the RNA-editing enzymes ADR-1 and/or ADR-2. Published viability assays demonstrate that each of the three genotypes (adr-1(−), adr-2(−), and the double mutant) exhibits comparably reduced thermotolerance, with approximately 80 % of animals failing to survive the heat shock. This value is obtained directly from the quantitative survival data reported for these alleles under the stated conditions and is therefore the single best match among the listed options.

B 

The question refers to a specific experimental evolution study involving Acinetobacter lwoffii (a close relative of the nosocomial pathogen A. baumannii). In that work, populations were serially passaged under increasing concentrations of the carbapenem meropenem until stable, high-level resistance emerged; the other listed agents were not the selective agents used in the published protocol. Therefore the single best choice is B.

C

The myotendinous junction (MTJ) is the specialized interface where muscle fibers attach to tendon extracellular matrix. Single-nucleus RNA-seq studies that transcriptionally profile myonuclei along the length of the myofiber consistently show that MTJ-resident myonuclei up-regulate genes encoding focal-adhesion proteins, integrins, collagen receptors, and other matrix-interacting molecules.  

- Option A (synaptic transmission) is the hallmark of neuromuscular-junction myonuclei, not MTJ nuclei.  
- Option B (signal transduction) is too generic and is not the dominant functional enrichment reported for MTJ nuclei.  
- Option D (protein synthesis) is broadly elevated in many myonuclear populations and does not specifically distinguish MTJ nuclei.  

Thus the single best answer is C.
